summary: Browser appears hung and vsync not occurring after wake from sleep after disconnecting external monitor on macOS
description:
After resume from sleep of an M3 MacBook Pro (November 2023) running macOS Tahoe 26.0 Beta (25A5346a), occasionally Nightly appears hung. For all windows, the tab bar is displayed as if the window is not the foreground window. Clicking on a different tab appears to have no effect, but after switching to another app and then back to Nightly, the new tab content is displayed. Using Activity Monitor to sample the parent process in the hung state show no instances of VsyncRefreshDriverTimer or nsRefreshDriver caught in the 1.9s sample.

After the hang was occurring, connecting an external monitor resulted in the browser being responsive to clicks again and work as normal without a restart of the browser.

Before the sleep, the laptop was connected to an external monitor and set to mirror mode. At some point, the monitor was disconnected and about 30 minutes later, the laptop was opened. I'm not sure if the computer was put to sleep while the monitor was still attached or after detaching.

comments.creator: bwerth@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Jamie Nicol [:jnicol] from comment #1)
> Any ideas how to investigate this further, Brad?

The problem will be somewhere in `OSXVsyncSource`. Here's some guesses as to what's going on:
1) We aren't [checking the return value of `CGDisplayRegisterReconfigurationCallback`](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/856a307913c2b73765b4e88d32cf15ed05549cae/gfx/thebes/gfxPlatformMac.cpp#737). That would put us in a situation where we get an initial vsync refresh, but then it stops working when displays are reconfigured. Is waking from sleep a reconfiguration event? Comments in the code imply that it is. So perhaps this is our problem. If this is the problem, then we need to figure out what to do in this situation, beyond emitting a warning that it is occurring.
2) Maybe we're hitting some of the `NS_WARNING` and that's not being reported adequately. We could try `gfxWarning()` instead and see if that makes failure messages more visible.

I'll put a patch together that does these things and we'll discuss in review.

comments.raw_text:
I was able to hit this problem with logging turned on (`gfx.logging.level=2` + Graphics preset), and captured the terminal output, MOZ_LOG output, and took screenshots of the logs when while Nightly was still in the error state. I didn't think to add timestamps to the logs so I'll retry with that enabled. The logs are huge and just the last sections are likely to be useful, but I've included them all at this [link](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/s7uyjyf5joo91ki1uhr86/2025-10-03-vsync-hang-raw-logs.zip?rlkey=dfbjc3elkq61up2kgaq38hat2&st=4pf5of32&dl=1). 40M compressed, 1GB decompressed.

In the MOZ_LOG output, the line `[Parent 75647: Main Thread]: D/nsRefreshDriver [1345d6200] Completed transaction id 5853` towards the end of the log might be a good reference point. When I resumed the machine and noticed the problem after clicking around in Nightly, that was one of the last lines printed when I checked the log. The log entries after that are from more clicking around, collecting a profile, and then plugging in a monitor which unstuck Nightly.

Files:
`MOZ_LOG-output-screenshot.png`:
Screenshot of the MOZ_LOG window tailing the log file while the issue was happening after resume from sleep

`vsync-debug-log.txt-main.75647.moz_log`:
MOZ_LOG output file

`Terminal-Output-Screenshot.png`:
Screenshot of the terminal output of Firefox

`terminal-output-log.txt`:
Terminal output

`profile_0_75647.json`:
A profile collected via signals while in the error state

The screenshots might be useful to understand which log messages occurred after resume from sleep. A few minutes after resuming from sleep, I plugged in an external monitor and then Nightly became responsive as usual.

I had two instances of Nightly running and both were in the hung state after this resume from sleep.

vim in the macOS Terminal couldn't handle the 800M log file, but VScode worked!

Hi! We’ve tried to reproduce this issue without success on macOS 26 mini M1 - Firefox 144.0, 145.0b2, and 146.0a1 (2025-10-16 - Using the Sleep option from the macOS menu:
1. Connected two monitors, then put macOS to sleep. While in sleep mode, disconnected one monitor and woke the system - all Firefox instances were working properly.
2. With one monitor connected, put macOS to sleep, then connected another monitor. After waking macOS, all Firefox instances were working.
3. With two monitors connected, I pressed the Sleep button, then disconnected one monitor. After waking up macOS, all Firefox instances were still working.

Also, the issue could not be reproduced by two other colleagues using different machines: Apple M1 – macOS 15.0 and MacBook Pro Apple M2 – macOS 26.1 Beta (25B5057f).

Let us know if you need any help!

comments.raw_text:
I think what is happening is this call pattern:
1) Monitor is disconnected, `OnDisplayReconfiguration` executes.
2) This calls `CreateDisplayLink`, which [fails](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/3aff965e839b7872bf48a9803b80669ca49276ac/gfx/thebes/gfxPlatformMac.cpp#801), nulls out the display link, emits a warning, and reschedules itself 100ms later using `RetryCreateDisplayLink`. It immediately returns.
3) `OnDisplayReconfiguration` [then calls `EnableVsync`](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/3aff965e839b7872bf48a9803b80669ca49276ac/gfx/thebes/gfxPlatformMac.cpp#956), which fails because the display link has been nulled out. It reschedules itself 100ms later using `RetryEnableVsync`.
4) This is all I can confirm from the logs in comment 18, so the rest is speculation...
5) The two previously scheduled calls arrive in the *opposite order*, and `EnableVsync` fails again because the display link is still null. The display link is never started.

If this is what's happening, then one way to fix it will be to create a new timed callback method `CreateDisplayLinkAndEnableVsync` which combines both methods -- same as they appear in `OnDisplayReconfiguration`. I'm going to try this out, as well as add a bit more `gfxWarning` logging.

comments.raw_text:
This changes a few things:
1) CreateDisplayLink loses its ability to reschedule itself. This is
because the rescheduling is only useful within the display
reconfiguration callback, and in such a case should be followed by a
call to EnableVsync.
2) A new retry callback CreateDisplayLinkAndEnableVsync is created to
handle the one-time retry within the display reconfiguration callback.
3) The OSXVsyncSource constructor no longer attempts to recreate the
display link if CreateDisplayLink fails. The only callsite for the
constructor will fallback to software vsync in such a case, and the
rescheduled attempt wasn't preventing that from happening anyway.
4) There are more gfxWarning messages, with more clarity on exactly
which call has failed.

This changes a few things:
1) CreateDisplayLink loses its ability to reschedule itself. This is
because the rescheduling is only useful within the display
reconfiguration callback, and in such a case should be followed by a
call to EnableVsync.
2) A new retry callback CreateDisplayLinkAndEnableVsync is created to
handle the one-time retry within the display reconfiguration callback.
3) The OSXVsyncSource constructor no longer attempts to recreate the
display link if CreateDisplayLink fails. The only callsite for the
constructor will fallback to software vsync in such a case, and the
rescheduled attempt wasn't preventing that from happening anyway.

> (In reply to Brad Werth [:bradwerth] from comment #20)
> 5) The two previously scheduled calls arrive in the *opposite order*, and `EnableVsync` fails again because the display link is still null. The display link is never started.

Rather, the [second callback overwrites the first](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/3aff965e839b7872bf48a9803b80669ca49276ac/xpcom/threads/nsTimerImpl.cpp#427), and the callback `RetryCreateDisplayLink` never fires. Later when `RetryEnableVsync` fires, it fails.

Regardless the proposed patch will also fix this behavior.

comments.text:
Thanks. Okay, the log shows that this is failing in `OSXVsyncSource::DisplayReconfigurationCallback`, and then failing again in the retry. But that doesn't make us fallback to software vsync, which is the part that's missing. I think we need a new part that when we fail in the callback, instead of retrying:
1) Call `OSXVsyncSource::Shutdown`.
2) Clear out the `gfxPlatform::mGlobalHardwareVsyncSource`. No method currently exists to do this.
3) Either immediately or after some delay, call `gfxPlatform::ReInitFrameRate`, which will attempt to setup hardware vsync again (sort of like doing our existing retry) and will fallback to software upon failure.

I'll build a new Part that does this.

Bug 1985140 Part 2: Make OSXVsyncSource safely retry vsync within display reconfiguration callback.


This changes a few things:
1) CreateDisplayLink loses its ability to reschedule itself. This is
because the rescheduling is only useful within the display
reconfiguration callback, and in such a case should be followed by a
call to EnableVsync.
2) A new retry callback CreateDisplayLinkAndEnableVsync is created to
handle the one-time retry within the display reconfiguration callback.
3) The OSXVsyncSource constructor no longer attempts to recreate the
display link if CreateDisplayLink fails. The only callsite for the
constructor will fallback to software vsync in such a case, and the
rescheduled attempt wasn't preventing that from happening anyway.
4) There are more gfxWarning messages, with more clarity on exactly
which call has failed.

Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D269119

Bug 1985140: Prevent OSXVsyncSource from creating a display link after it fails to register a reconfiguration callback.


This also changes NS_WARNING to gfxWarning(), in the hope of making serious vysnc failures more
visible.

Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D262819
comments.author: bwerth@mozilla.com

comments.creator: bwerth@mozilla.com
bwerth@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
This changes a few things:
1) CreateDisplayLink loses its ability to reschedule itself. This is
because the rescheduling is only useful within the display
reconfiguration callback, and in such a case should be followed by a
call to EnableVsync.
2) A new retry callback CreateDisplayLinkAndEnableVsync is created to
handle the one-time retry within the display reconfiguration callback.
3) The OSXVsyncSource constructor no longer attempts to recreate the
display link if CreateDisplayLink fails. The only callsite for the
constructor will fallback to software vsync in such a case, and the
rescheduled attempt wasn't preventing that from happening anyway.
4) There are more gfxWarning messages, with more clarity on exactly
which call has failed.

Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D269119

### firefox-esr140 Uplift Approval Request
- **User impact if declined**: Seems to affect many users. If user connects or disconnects external monitor when device is asleep, Firefox will be unresponsive until it is restarted.
- **Code covered by automated testing**: no
- **Fix verified in Nightly**: yes
- **Needs manual QE test**: yes
- **Steps to reproduce for manual QE testing**: QA has been able to verify so far, and should continue to use that method. My summary:
attach external monitor to laptop
close laptop lid
wait for laptop sleep mode to engage (10 seconds?)
detach external monitor
open laptop lid
- **Risk associated with taking this patch**: low
- **Explanation of risk level**: Activates a working fallback in an existing error case, rather than silently failing.
- **String changes made/needed**: None
- **Is Android affected?**: no

comments.text:
Created attachment 9538009
Bug 1985140 Part 3: Make OSXVsyncSource give up when display reconfiguration fails.


This adds a method gfxPlatform::ResetHardwareVsyncSource to allow a
hardware vsync source to remove itself when needed. The OSXVsyncSource
now calls this method when display reconfiguration fails, and the
time-delayed retry *also* fails. This allows a software vsync method to
be used as a fallback, ensuring that some vsync source is active.

Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D273131
